title: Concurrency Budgets, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Worker Threads · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in admission, concurrency, queue, rate, and CPU-work budget and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.
Bound active requests, per-dependency concurrency, queue length, queue age, memory, rejection, Retry-After, and fairness separately.
Apply rate and quota policies to trusted identities, tenant and operation cost, distributed storage, clock behavior, headers, exemptions, and failure mode.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Event Loop Lag Blocking
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 8885 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure CPU blocking, synchronous I/O, long callbacks, and event-loop delay.

Inspect the Event Loop Lag Blocking trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Event Loop Lag Blocking.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish asynchronous concurrency from parallelism and measure cpu blocking, synchronous i/o, long callbacks, and event-loop delay. For Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Event Loop Lag Blocking, keep Event Loop Lag Blocking connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Overload Recovery Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: httpsts-overload-recovery-evidence-cb9fz7" that uses case 2504 to combine concurrency, queue, tenant-rate, and bounded CPU-offload decisions. Browser code must not import Node or Express, open listeners, contact networks or databases, spawn workers, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24 and Express 5 transfer gates.
